---
title: "SSE Karnel"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
rm(list = ls())

library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)


############################################################
# MODEL SETTINGS
############################################################

m <- 57
hours <- 24

v_min <- 2^(-5)
delta_v <- 0.125

size_index <- 1:m

v_i <- v_min * 2^((size_index - 1) * delta_v)


############################################################
# TRUE PARAMETER VALUES
############################################################

gamma_max_true <- 0.20
delta_max_true <- 0.03
E_star_true <- 500
a_true <- 2
b_true <- 1

# Kernel memory-decay parameter
p_true <- 0.40

# Maximum number of previous hours included
# This is fixed, not estimated
tau_max <- 33


true_base <- c(
  gamma_max = gamma_max_true,
  delta_max = delta_max_true,
  E_star    = E_star_true,
  a         = a_true,
  b         = b_true,
  p         = p_true
)


############################################################
# LIGHT FUNCTION
############################################################

E_t <- function(t) {
  
  # Repeats the light cycle every 24 hours
  t_mod <- t %% 24
  
  light <- 1000 * max(
    0,
    sin(2 * pi * t_mod / 24)
  )
  
  return(light)
}


############################################################
# GEOMETRIC KERNEL WEIGHTS
############################################################

kernel_weights <- function(p, tau_max) {
  
  # Prevent p from becoming exactly zero or one
  p <- min(
    max(p, 1e-8),
    1 - 1e-8
  )
  
  tau_values <- 0:tau_max
  
  weights <- p * (1 - p)^tau_values
  
  # Normalize so that the weights add to one
  weights <- weights / sum(weights)
  
  return(weights)
}


############################################################
# EFFECTIVE LIGHT USING THE KERNEL
############################################################

E_kernel <- function(t, p, tau_max) {
  
  tau_values <- 0:tau_max
  
  weights <- kernel_weights(
    p = p,
    tau_max = tau_max
  )
  
  light_values <- sapply(
    tau_values,
    function(tau) {
      E_t(t - tau)
    }
  )
  
  chi_t <- sum(
    weights * light_values
  )
  
  return(chi_t)
}


############################################################
# KERNEL-DEPENDENT GROWTH FUNCTION
############################################################

gamma_t <- function(
    t,
    gamma_max,
    E_star,
    p,
    tau_max
) {
  
  chi_t <- E_kernel(
    t = t,
    p = p,
    tau_max = tau_max
  )
  
  gamma <- gamma_max *
    (1 - exp(-chi_t / E_star))
  
  return(gamma)
}


############################################################
# DIVISION FUNCTION
############################################################

delta_i_t <- function(t, delta_max, a, b) {
  
  # No division before hour 6
  if (t < 6) {
    return(rep(0, m))
  }
  
  delta_i <- delta_max *
    ((a * v_i)^b /
       (1 + (a * v_i)^b))
  
  return(delta_i)
}


############################################################
# MATRIX A(t)
############################################################

matrix_A <- function(
    t,
    gamma_max,
    delta_max,
    E_star,
    a,
    b,
    p,
    tau_max
) {
  
  A <- matrix(
    0,
    nrow = m,
    ncol = m
  )
  
  # Kernel-dependent growth rate
  gamma <- gamma_t(
    t = t,
    gamma_max = gamma_max,
    E_star = E_star,
    p = p,
    tau_max = tau_max
  )
  
  # Size-dependent division probabilities
  delta_i <- delta_i_t(
    t = t,
    delta_max = delta_max,
    a = a,
    b = b
  )
  
  # Number of size classes corresponding to doubling
  j <- as.integer(
    1 + 1 / delta_v
  )
  
  
  ##########################################################
  # GROWTH
  ##########################################################
  
  # Cells move to the next larger size class
  
  for (i in 1:(m - 1)) {
    
    A[i + 1, i] <-
      A[i + 1, i] +
      gamma * (1 - delta_i[i])
  }
  
  
  # Cells in the largest size class remain there
  # if they grow without dividing
  
  A[m, m] <-
    A[m, m] +
    gamma * (1 - delta_i[m])
  
  
  ##########################################################
  # DIVISION
  ##########################################################
  
  # Smaller cells produce daughters in size class 1
  
  for (i in 2:(j - 1)) {
    
    A[1, i] <-
      A[1, i] +
      2 * delta_i[i]
  }
  
  
  # Larger cells produce daughters approximately
  # half the size of the parent
  
  for (i in j:m) {
    
    smaller_class <- i + 1 - j
    
    A[smaller_class, i] <-
      A[smaller_class, i] +
      2 * delta_i[i]
  }
  
  
  ##########################################################
  # STASIS
  ##########################################################
  
  # Size class 1
  
  A[1, 1] <-
    A[1, 1] +
    ((1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[1])) +
    2 * delta_i[1]
  
  
  # Interior size classes
  
  for (i in 2:(m - 1)) {
    
    A[i, i] <-
      A[i, i] +
      (1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[i])
  }
  
  
  # Largest size class
  
  A[m, m] <-
    A[m, m] +
    (1 - gamma) * (1 - delta_i[m])
  
  
  return(A)
}


############################################################
# MATRIX B(t): ONE-HOUR TRANSITION MATRIX
############################################################

matrix_B <- function(
    t,
    gamma_max,
    delta_max,
    E_star,
    a,
    b,
    p,
    tau_max,
    dt = 1 / 6
) {
  
  # Begin with the 57 by 57 identity matrix
  B <- diag(m)
  
  # Six ten-minute matrices per hour
  n_steps <- as.integer(
    round(1 / dt)
  )
  
  for (k in 0:(n_steps - 1)) {
    
    time_now <- t + k * dt
    
    A_k <- matrix_A(
      t = time_now,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b,
      p = p,
      tau_max = tau_max
    )
    
    B <- A_k %*% B
  }
  
  return(B)
}


############################################################
# INITIAL SIZE DISTRIBUTION
############################################################

w0 <- dnorm(
  size_index,
  mean = 20,
  sd = 6
)

w0 <- w0 / sum(w0)


plot(
  v_i,
  w0,
  type = "l",
  lwd = 2,
  xlab = "Cell volume",
  ylab = "Initial proportion",
  main = "Initial Size Distribution"
)


############################################################
# SIMULATE THE KERNEL MODEL
############################################################

simulate_model <- function(params) {
  
  gamma_max <- params[["gamma_max"]]
  delta_max <- params[["delta_max"]]
  E_star <- params[["E_star"]]
  a <- params[["a"]]
  b <- params[["b"]]
  p <- params[["p"]]
  
  W <- matrix(
    0,
    nrow = hours,
    ncol = m
  )
  
  # First row represents hour zero
  W[1, ] <- w0
  
  for (row in 1:(hours - 1)) {
    
    # First transition starts at hour zero
    time_start <- row - 1
    
    B_t <- matrix_B(
      t = time_start,
      gamma_max = gamma_max,
      delta_max = delta_max,
      E_star = E_star,
      a = a,
      b = b,
      p = p,
      tau_max = tau_max
    )
    
    W[row + 1, ] <-
      B_t %*% W[row, ]
    
    # Normalize to obtain proportions
    W[row + 1, ] <-
      W[row + 1, ] /
      sum(W[row + 1, ])
  }
  
  return(W)
}


############################################################
# HEATMAP FUNCTION
############################################################

plot_heatmap <- function(
    W,
    title_name,
    maximum_proportion = max(W)
) {
  
  df <- melt(W)
  
  colnames(df) <- c(
    "Hour",
    "SizeClass",
    "Proportion"
  )
  
  # Matrix row 1 represents hour zero
  df$Hour <- df$Hour - 1
  
  df$CellVolume <- v_i[df$SizeClass]
  
  heatmap_plot <- ggplot(
    df,
    aes(
      x = CellVolume,
      y = Hour,
      fill = Proportion
    )
  ) +
    geom_tile() +
    
    scale_x_log10() +
    
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = seq(0, 23, by = 3)
    ) +
    
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors = c(
        "blue",
        "cyan",
        "yellow",
        "red"
      ),
      limits = c(0, maximum_proportion)
    ) +
    
    labs(
      title = title_name,
      subtitle = paste0(
        "p = ",
        p_true,
        ", tau_max = ",
        tau_max
      ),
      x = expression(
        "Cell volume (" * mu * m^3 * ")"
      ),
      y = "Hour after dawn",
      fill = "Proportion"
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(heatmap_plot)
}


############################################################
# GENERATE THE TRUE KERNEL HEATMAP
############################################################

W_true <- simulate_model(
  params = true_base
)


# Check that every hourly distribution adds to one

print(
  rowSums(W_true)
)


############################################################
# CREATE FAKE OBSERVATIONS
############################################################

set.seed(123)

N <- 1000000

Counts <- matrix(
  0,
  nrow = hours,
  ncol = m
)


for (t in 1:hours) {
  
  Counts[t, ] <- rmultinom(
    n = 1,
    size = N,
    prob = W_true[t, ]
  )
}


W_obs <- Counts / rowSums(Counts)


############################################################
# PLOT TRUE AND FAKE OBSERVED HEATMAPS
############################################################

common_heatmap_max <- max(
  W_true,
  W_obs
)


p_true_heatmap <- plot_heatmap(
  W = W_true,
  title_name = "True Kernel Model Heatmap",
  maximum_proportion = common_heatmap_max
)


p_observed_heatmap <- plot_heatmap(
  W = W_obs,
  title_name = "Fake Observed Kernel Heatmap",
  maximum_proportion = common_heatmap_max
)


print(p_true_heatmap)
print(p_observed_heatmap)


############################################################
# SSE OBJECTIVE-SURFACE ANALYSIS
############################################################


############################################################
# STEP 1: PARAMETER GRIDS
############################################################

# Six parameters produce 15 unique parameter pairs.
#
# You can temporarily use fewer grid points to test the code:
# grid_points <- 7

grid_points <- 15


param_ranges <- list(
  
  gamma_max = seq(
    0.10,
    0.30,
    length.out = grid_points
  ),
  
  delta_max = seq(
    0.01,
    0.08,
    length.out = grid_points
  ),
  
  E_star = seq(
    100,
    1000,
    length.out = grid_points
  ),
  
  a = seq(
    0.5,
    4,
    length.out = grid_points
  ),
  
  b = seq(
    0.5,
    4,
    length.out = grid_points
  ),
  
  p = seq(
    0.10,
    0.80,
    length.out = grid_points
  )
)


############################################################
# STEP 2: CALCULATE SUM OF SQUARED ERROR
############################################################

calculate_sse <- function(W_model, W_obs) {
  
  error <- W_obs - W_model
  
  sse <- sum(error^2)
  
  return(sse)
}


############################################################
# STEP 3: CREATE ONE TWO-PARAMETER OBJECTIVE SURFACE
############################################################

make_objective_surface <- function(param1, param2) {
  
  number_of_combinations <-
    length(param_ranges[[param1]]) *
    length(param_ranges[[param2]])
  
  surface_rows <- vector(
    mode = "list",
    length = number_of_combinations
  )
  
  result_number <- 1
  
  
  for (value1 in param_ranges[[param1]]) {
    
    for (value2 in param_ranges[[param2]]) {
      
      # Begin with all parameters at their true values
      test_params <- true_base
      
      # Change only the two selected parameters
      test_params[param1] <- value1
      test_params[param2] <- value2
      
      # Simulate the kernel model
      W_model <- simulate_model(
        params = test_params
      )
      
      # Compare the model with the fake observations
      sse_value <- calculate_sse(
        W_model = W_model,
        W_obs = W_obs
      )
      
      surface_rows[[result_number]] <- data.frame(
        
        Pair = paste(
          param1,
          param2,
          sep = "_vs_"
        ),
        
        Param1 = param1,
        Param2 = param2,
        
        Param1Value = value1,
        Param2Value = value2,
        
        SSE = sse_value,
        Log10SSE = log10(sse_value)
      )
      
      print(
        paste(
          "Finished:",
          param1,
          "=",
          round(value1, 4),
          ",",
          param2,
          "=",
          round(value2, 4),
          "| SSE =",
          signif(sse_value, 6)
        )
      )
      
      result_number <- result_number + 1
    }
  }
  
  surface_results <- do.call(
    rbind,
    surface_rows
  )
  
  return(surface_results)
}


############################################################
# STEP 4: CREATE ALL PARAMETER PAIRS
############################################################

parameter_names <- names(true_base)


pair_list <- combn(
  parameter_names,
  2,
  simplify = FALSE
)


print(pair_list)


############################################################
# STEP 5: CALCULATE ALL 15 OBJECTIVE SURFACES
############################################################

all_surface_list <- vector(
  mode = "list",
  length = length(pair_list)
)


for (pair_number in seq_along(pair_list)) {
  
  current_pair <- pair_list[[pair_number]]
  
  param1 <- current_pair[1]
  param2 <- current_pair[2]
  
  cat(
    "\nStarting objective surface:",
    param1,
    "vs",
    param2,
    "\n"
  )
  
  all_surface_list[[pair_number]] <-
    make_objective_surface(
      param1 = param1,
      param2 = param2
    )
}


all_surface_results <- do.call(
  rbind,
  all_surface_list
)


############################################################
# STEP 6: VIEW AND SAVE ALL SSE VALUES
############################################################

print(all_surface_results)


write.csv(
  all_surface_results,
  "kernel_objective_surface_results.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)


############################################################
# STEP 7: FUNCTION TO PLOT ONE OBJECTIVE SURFACE
############################################################

make_surface_plot <- function(
    pair_name,
    use_log = TRUE
) {
  
  plot_data <- subset(
    all_surface_results,
    Pair == pair_name
  )
  
  param1_name <- unique(
    plot_data$Param1
  )
  
  param2_name <- unique(
    plot_data$Param2
  )
  
  true_x <- unname(
    true_base[param1_name]
  )
  
  true_y <- unname(
    true_base[param2_name]
  )
  
  
  if (use_log == TRUE) {
    
    fill_variable <- "Log10SSE"
    fill_title <- expression(log[10]("SSE"))
    subtitle_text <- "Color represents log10(SSE)"
    
  } else {
    
    fill_variable <- "SSE"
    fill_title <- "SSE"
    subtitle_text <- "Color represents SSE"
  }
  
  
  surface_plot <- ggplot(
    plot_data,
    aes(
      x = Param1Value,
      y = Param2Value,
      fill = .data[[fill_variable]]
    )
  ) +
    
    geom_tile() +
    
    # Black star shows the true parameter pair
    
    geom_point(
      data = data.frame(
        TrueX = true_x,
        TrueY = true_y
      ),
      aes(
        x = TrueX,
        y = TrueY
      ),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      shape = 8,
      size = 4,
      color = "black"
    ) +
    
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors = c(
        "blue",
        "cyan",
        "yellow",
        "red"
      )
    ) +
    
    labs(
      title = paste(
        "Kernel objective surface:",
        param1_name,
        "vs",
        param2_name
      ),
      subtitle = paste(
        subtitle_text,
        "| black star = true values"
      ),
      x = paste(
        "Value of",
        param1_name
      ),
      y = paste(
        "Value of",
        param2_name
      ),
      fill = fill_title
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(
        face = "bold"
      )
    )
  
  return(surface_plot)
}


############################################################
# STEP 8: PLOT AND SAVE ALL OBJECTIVE SURFACES
############################################################

pair_names <- unique(
  all_surface_results$Pair
)


surface_plots <- list()


for (pair_name in pair_names) {
  
  surface_plot <- make_surface_plot(
    pair_name = pair_name,
    use_log = TRUE
  )
  
  surface_plots[[pair_name]] <- surface_plot
  
  print(surface_plot)
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0(
      "kernel_objective_surface_",
      pair_name,
      ".png"
    ),
    plot = surface_plot,
    width = 7,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}


############################################################
# STEP 9: FIND THE SMALLEST SSE FOR EACH PARAMETER PAIR
############################################################

best_grid_rows <- vector(
  mode = "list",
  length = length(pair_names)
)


for (pair_number in seq_along(pair_names)) {
  
  pair_name <- pair_names[pair_number]
  
  pair_data <- subset(
    all_surface_results,
    Pair == pair_name
  )
  
  best_grid_rows[[pair_number]] <-
    pair_data[
      which.min(pair_data$SSE),
    ]
}


best_grid_points <- do.call(
  rbind,
  best_grid_rows
)


############################################################
# STEP 10: PRINT AND SAVE THE BEST GRID POINTS
############################################################

print(
  "Best grid point for each parameter pair:"
)

print(best_grid_points)


write.csv(
  best_grid_points,
  "kernel_best_grid_points.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)


############################################################
# STEP 11: COMPARE BEST POINTS WITH TRUE VALUES
############################################################

best_grid_summary_rows <- vector(
  mode = "list",
  length = nrow(best_grid_points)
)


for (row_number in seq_len(nrow(best_grid_points))) {
  
  current_row <- best_grid_points[row_number, ]
  
  param1_name <- current_row$Param1
  param2_name <- current_row$Param2
  
  best_grid_summary_rows[[row_number]] <- data.frame(
    
    Pair = current_row$Pair,
    
    Param1 = param1_name,
    Param1True = unname(
      true_base[param1_name]
    ),
    Param1Best = current_row$Param1Value,
    
    Param2 = param2_name,
    Param2True = unname(
      true_base[param2_name]
    ),
    Param2Best = current_row$Param2Value,
    
    MinimumSSE = current_row$SSE
  )
}


best_grid_summary <- do.call(
  rbind,
  best_grid_summary_rows
)


print(
  "Comparison of the best grid points with the true values:"
)

print(best_grid_summary)


write.csv(
  best_grid_summary,
  "kernel_best_grid_summary.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
